title: Transparency as a Way of Attaining Quality, Safety and Optimal Food Purchases

summary.about: How food transparency, labels and trust affect consumers’ purchasing decisions.
summary.audience: Food retailers, regulators, brands and consumer-behavior researchers.
summary.method: Combines a literature review with six semi-structured consumer interviews in Washington State.
summary.insights: Consumers favor organic and certified food and want transparency, but often rely on assumptions, stores and labels rather than source knowledge.
